reproducibility: Each entry pins a real repo + vulnerable/patched commit SHAs; the detector runs over the real target file(s) fetched at those SHAs. Pin-or-drop: an entry that cannot be resolved to fetchable ground truth is dropped from the denominator with a reason, never counted missed, never hand-faked.

result.measured_on: real target files at pinned vuln/patched SHAs (line-precise: a FLAGGED line's own text contains the sink probe in vuln, not in patched)
result.miss_note: langchain CVE-2023-46229 patches via prevent_outside (a same-domain bool flag), not a validation call, so its sink still flags in the patched tree -> documented miss / future improvement-loop fixture.

seed_swap.reason: Its authoritative NVD weakness is CWE-601 (a redirect-mitigation bypass / open redirect), NOT CWE-918 SSRF (taint from an untrusted source to an outbound-request sink). Per the verify-at-build contract, a seed whose authoritative class does not match its row is swapped for a genuine public example of the class before calibration.

seed.source: rel.reltype — an external-relationship URL embedded in an attacker-uploaded DOCX, assigned to `url` in DocxExtractor._extract_images_from_docx
seed.sink: requests.get(url, stream=True) — outbound HTTP with the raw DOCX-supplied URL, no host/scheme allowlist or private-IP block
seed.patched_shape: sink substituted to the SSRF-hardened proxy: requests.get(url, stream=True) -> ssrf_proxy.get(url, stream=True) (core.helper.ssrf_proxy)

heldout.sink: httpx sync_client.stream("GET", url, follow_redirects=True) / async_client.stream in save_url_to_cache
heldout.patched_shape: check_public_url(url) guard (scheme http/https + socket.getaddrinfo + ipaddress.is_global) before the request and on every redirect hop

heldout.sink: await client.get(item.url, follow_redirects=True) in download_item() (httpx AsyncClient)
heldout.patched_shape: safe_download() with scheme restriction, DNS pre-resolution (anti-rebinding), private/internal + cloud-metadata IP block (new _ssrf.py)

heldout.sink: requests.get(url, timeout=..., headers=...) and aiohttp session.get(url) in RecursiveUrlLoader
heldout.patched_shape: same-domain restriction: prevent_outside made a real bool defaulting True so off-domain recursively-discovered links are filtered before fetch (weaker guard — a same-domain filter, not an allowlist/IP block)
heldout.notes: weaker patched guard (bool flag, not a validation call) — may be a harder discrimination case

heldout.sink: client.get(url_spec.geturl(), headers=..., timeout=...) where client = requests.Session(); url_spec from a user-supplied media URL
heldout.patched_shape: validation added before the fetch (sink line remains client.get); confirmed at build

denominator_note: Generalization denominator = 4 pinned held-out CVEs (gradio, pydantic-ai, langchain, lmdeploy). All genuine Python CWE-918 SSRFs verified on NVD, distinct sinks (requests/httpx/aiohttp) and guards (proxy-substitution/validation-call/IP-block/same-domain) so a seed signature would not generalize.
